channel.title: NPR Programs: All Things Considered
channel.description: All Things Considered hosts Ailsa Chang, Mary Louise Kelly, Juana Summers and Scott Detrow present the program's trademark mix of news, interviews, commentaries, reviews, and offbeat features 7 days a week.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

item.title: New York Times reports sexual abuse allegations against Cesar Chavez
item.description: A New York Times investigation has revealed allegations that the late renowned labor leader Cesar Chavez abused girls and raped Dolores Huerta, his longtime organizing partner.
item.content:encoded: A New York Times investigation has revealed allegations that the late renowned labor leader Cesar Chavez abused girls and raped Dolores Huerta, his longtime organizing partner.
item.dc:creator: Adrian Florido

item.title: Senate Intelligence Committee questions national security chiefs about the Iran war
item.description: The nation's top intelligence official, Tulsi Gabbard, said today that Iran's government still seems to be functioning, though it has been greatly weakened by the U.S. and Israeli bombing campaign.
item.content:encoded: The nation's top intelligence official, Tulsi Gabbard, said today that Iran's government still seems to be functioning, though it has been greatly weakened by the U.S. and Israeli bombing campaign.
item.dc:creator: Greg Myre

item.title: Tracing recent events that led Netanyahu to launch the war against Iran
item.description: A chain of events in recent years led Israel and the U.S. to do what they had hesitated to do for decades: launch all-out war against Iran.
item.content:encoded: A chain of events in recent years led Israel and the U.S. to do what they had hesitated to do for decades: launch all-out war against Iran.
item.dc:creator: Daniel Estrin

item.title: Federal Reserve holds a key interest rate steady amid growing economic pressures
item.description: The Federal Reserve held its benchmark interest rate steady today. The central bank is wrestling with a lot of uncertainty with a weak job market and stubborn inflation.
item.content:encoded: The Federal Reserve held its benchmark interest rate steady today. The central bank is wrestling with a lot of uncertainty with a weak job market and stubborn inflation.
item.dc:creator: Scott Horsley

item.title: KiKi Shepard, an avatar of elegance on 'Showtime at the Apollo,' has died
item.description: KiKi Shepard, the longtime co-host of Showtime at the Apollo, died this week at 74.
item.content:encoded: KiKi Shepard, the longtime co-host of Showtime at the Apollo, died this week at 74.
item.dc:creator: Neda Ulaby

item.title: Former federal advisers on autism who were let go by RFK Jr. form a new committee
item.description: An independent group of experts plans to offer an alternative to the Trump administration's autism agenda. The group features prominent scientists who used to serve on a federal advisory committee.
item.content:encoded: An independent group of experts plans to offer an alternative to the Trump administration's autism agenda. The group features prominent scientists who used to serve on a federal advisory committee.
item.dc:creator: Jon Hamilton

item.title: Former Syrian prison chief convicted in landmark torture trial in LA
item.description: A former Syrian prison chief has been convicted in a landmark torture trial in Los Angeles.
item.content:encoded: A former Syrian prison chief has been convicted in a landmark torture trial in Los Angeles.
item.dc:creator: Deborah Amos

item.title: WNBA players union and owners reach a verbal agreement
item.description: NPR's Ailsa Chang talks with Meghan Hall from USA Today's For the Win about negotiations between WNBA players and owners on a new collective bargaining agreement.
item.content:encoded: NPR's Ailsa Chang talks with Meghan Hall from USA Today's For the Win about negotiations between WNBA players and owners on a new collective bargaining agreement.
item.dc:creator: Ailsa Chang

item.title: Iran war hinders the flow of U.N. aid through the Gulf to communities in need
item.description: It's not just oil tankers stranded near the Strait of Hormuz. U.N. aid shipments are also hindered by the war in Iran.
item.content:encoded: It's not just oil tankers stranded near the Strait of Hormuz. U.N. aid shipments are also hindered by the war in Iran.
item.dc:creator: Aya Batrawy

item.title: Gas price spike spurs a political blame game in California
item.description: California's gas prices have long been high, and spikes due to the war with Iran have sparked debate over who's to blame. Some Democrats say Gov. Gavin Newsom could do more to ease prices.
item.content:encoded: California's gas prices have long been high, and spikes due to the war with Iran have sparked debate over who's to blame. Some Democrats say Gov. Gavin Newsom could do more to ease prices.
item.dc:creator: Guy Marzorati
